shared_components.name: Header
shared_components.purpose: Provides main site navigation with brand, menu links, and quick auth trigger.

shared_components.name: HeroSection
shared_components.purpose: Immediately grabs interest, conveys GymFlow's value and AI focus, and drives direct calls to action.

shared_components.name: FeaturesSection
shared_components.purpose: Showcase AI-powered feature set clearly with visual appeal.

shared_components.name: HowItWorksSection
shared_components.purpose: Illustrates GymFlow's onboarding simplicity and intelligent workflow.

shared_components.name: PricingSection
shared_components.purpose: Clearly describes value/feature difference for each plan, drives signups.

shared_components.name: TestimonialsSection
shared_components.purpose: Builds social proof through realistic, AI-focused gym owner feedback.

shared_components.name: FAQSection
shared_components.purpose: Addresses pre-signup concerns, shows GymFlow's expertise.

shared_components.name: CTASection
shared_components.purpose: Drives conversion with compelling final CTA.

shared_components.name: Footer
shared_components.purpose: Provides branding, navigation, and legal links.

shared_components.name: LoginModal
shared_components.purpose: Allows gym users to log in securely.

shared_components.name: RegisterModal
shared_components.purpose: Enables new gym owners to join GymFlow.

shared_components.name: ForgotPasswordModal
shared_components.purpose: Allows users to reset their password.

shared_components.name: AuthModal
shared_components.purpose: App-wide login/signup dialog. This is the SINGLE auth surface — no /login route exists in this project.

layout_plan.composition: Asymmetric hero: oversized headline top-left with a primary CTA; a live metric/stat tile anchored bottom-right. Not centered.
Modular grid of UNEQUAL tiles — one large feature tile spanning 2 columns/rows, plus smaller tiles (a stat, a short quote, an image, a mini-feature). Varied sizes, never a uniform 3-up.
Wide product/feature band: large mockup or visual on a tinted surface, supporting copy beside it.
Single full-width row: logo strip or 3–4 large stat numerals with small labels.
Full-width CTA band, left-aligned headline + button on a brand-tinted surface.

notes:
TYPOGRAPHY: display font "Bricolage Grotesque" for headings/hero, body font "Inter" for body/labels — load both from Google Fonts via a <link> in index.html and apply with Tailwind arbitrary classes (font-['Bricolage Grotesque'] / font-['Inter']). Do NOT substitute DM Serif Display or a generic system font.

Design DNA: Modern Premium SaaS Dashboard. Glassmorphism cards on dark backgrounds (#0F172A), primary emerald (#10B981), secondary slate, UI chrome with backdrops. Large sans-serif font, bold weights. Elevation/shadows for glass cards. Rich, professional iconography. Subtle but confident motion.. Animation: Framer Motion - smooth fade, scale, slide; buttons with soft hover accent. Interaction: Smooth, polished, modal opening/closing via Framer Motion, glass cards with direct feedback.. Color scheme: dark.

LAYOUT ARCHETYPE: bento — build each page from its layout_plan; do NOT collapse into a centered hero followed by a uniform 3-card grid and footer. A card grid, if used, is at most ONE section among differently-composed sections.

REFERENCE DESIGN SYSTEM: supabase — this is THIS project's design system. USE its palette (tokens.colors above are taken from it) and its font stacks. DISTILL its component treatments (buttons, cards, nav), type scale + tracking, spacing rhythm, and depth/shadow system, and match its level of polish. Do NOT fall back to generic defaults (raw Tailwind gray text, #3B82F6 blue, system fonts) or invent an unrelated purple/dark theme. Brief follows:

---
version: alpha
name: Supabaze-Inspired-design-analysis
description: An inspired interpretation of Supabaze's design language — an open-source database platform built on a clean white-and-near-black system with a single signature emerald-green CTA, a custom humanist sans display tier, and dense product UI mockups composited above the hero. The brand reads as quietly technical: minimal chrome, a near-monochrome palette, and the green primary acting as the only chromatic event on the page.

## Overview

Supabaze's design language is engineered for clarity above all else. The marketing surfaces sit on `{colors.canvas}` (pure white), with text rendered in `{colors.ink}` (`#171717` — near-black, never pure black). Across the entire system the only consistent chromatic event is the **emerald green primary** (`{colors.primary}` — `#3ecf8e`) — used as the filled CTA, occasional accent dot, and the signature highlight color in the wordmark. Everything else is a calibrated grey ladder from `#ededed` hairline-cool to `#171717` ink, with thin black-on-white typography doing most of the visual work.
